quotes: the callout sits on the page/section background (usually White `#ffffff`). The blue box itself is inset from the left by half the badge width
vertically centered on the box and horizontally centered on the box's left edge
Let W = badge width (use 80px, icon ?48px inside).
Inset the blue box from the left by W/2 = 40px
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.
Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow stays identical
inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2 + 24px, badge margin-top = ?H/2

rules.rule_text: The icon callout box must follow strict geometric and structural rules: the white icon badge sits 50% overhanging the box edge (inset from left by half the badge width W/2 = 40px), vertically centered. Every instance of the badge must feature a subtle drop shadow (0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18)) to avoid appearing flat. The box container features a 40px 8px 40px 8px radius.

quotes: pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG
When that's not feasible, ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at the inner-left edge)

rules.rule_text: To guarantee perfect layout fidelity on platforms that ignore absolute positioning (such as Outlook desktop), pre-composite the white badge with its shadow and radii as a transparent PNG, or bake it directly into the box's background image. If live layout is implemented instead, accept the degraded layout in Outlook (badge flush at the inner-left edge) where the Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, and blue text styling remain identical.

quotes: every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical
every other rule (Light Blue fill, `40px 8px 40px 8px` radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.
